RadScheduler

Radiology Practice Scheduler

Complete shift management for diagnostic and interventional radiology practices — with anchor site enforcement, multi-year holiday tracking, and role-based access.

Anchor site compliance (≥60% enforcement)
Mixed DR/IR FTE with per-shift eligibility
IR auto-assign by North/South site group
Holiday call counter across multiple years
Admin & physician user roles

📲 Calendar Sync
📥 Download as .ics file
Get a one-time snapshot of your schedule as an iCalendar file. Open it in Apple Calendar, Outlook, or import into Google Calendar to add all your shifts at once. Doesn't auto-update — re-download anytime your schedule changes.
🔗 Subscribe (auto-updating)
Add a subscription URL to your calendar app. Your phone or computer's calendar will check for updates periodically (typically every few hours) and pull in any schedule changes automatically — no need to re-download.
How to use it:
  • iPhone / Apple Calendar: Settings → Calendar → Accounts → Add Account → Other → Add Subscribed Calendar → paste URL
  • Google Calendar: calendar.google.com → "Other calendars" + → "From URL" → paste
  • Outlook: Calendar → Add calendar → Subscribe from web → paste URL
⚠ Privacy: This URL contains a token that lets anyone view your schedule. Don't share it. To revoke access (if leaked), click "Generate URL" again — old URL stops working.
User Profiles
All registered accounts in this practice.
🔔 Preferences
Personal settings — notification routing, display size, and delivery email.
📲 Push notifications on this device
Get notified the moment a shift is assigned to you, a swap is approved, or you've been mentioned in chat — even when RadScheduler isn't open. Requires the service worker (Settings → Offline support → Download sw.js) and a one-time browser permission.
📅 Add to your calendar app
Download a one-time .ics file containing every assignment on your schedule. Open it in Apple Calendar, Google Calendar, Outlook, or Fantastical and the events appear alongside everything else.
🔠 Display Size
Applied immediately. Saved to your account so it syncs across your devices.
Delivery Address
If left blank, alerts go to your login email. Use this field if your login email isn't a real inbox (e.g., admin@rad.com) — set this to your personal or work email (like Gmail) to actually receive alerts.
🔐 Change Password
Update the password you use to sign in. Minimum 8 characters. You'll stay signed in on this device — no need to log out.

Click this to send yourself a test email and confirm that notifications are wired up end-to-end. If nothing arrives, check the browser console (F12) and your spam folder.
📢 Broadcast Email
Send an email to everyone in this practice. Recipients who have disabled "Practice-wide announcements" in their preferences will not receive this message.

▶ One-time setup: deploy the send-notification edge function
To actually send emails, you need to deploy a Supabase Edge Function that connects to an email provider (recommended: Resend — free tier covers 3,000 emails/month and setup is minimal).
  1. Create an account at resend.com and verify your sending domain (or use their onboarding domain for testing).
  2. Copy your Resend API key.
  3. In your Supabase dashboard → Edge Functions → Create function → name it send-notification.
  4. Paste the Deno code below.
  5. In the function's Secrets, add:
    RESEND_API_KEY = your Resend API key
    FROM_EMAIL = e.g. scheduler@yourdomain.com (must be a verified sender)
  6. Deploy the function.
Deno source code:
Loading…
💡 Tip: Once deployed, notifications from chat, swaps, and schedule changes will start flowing automatically — each gated by the recipient's preferences. If the function isn't deployed, the app still works; notifications just silently skip.
🔬 Tumor Boards & Recurring Conferences
Define recurring multidisciplinary conferences. Each board sets a weekly time slot, the sites and subspecialties it serves, and the physicians who participate. Boards appear automatically on each participant's downloaded .ics calendar feed and (if enabled) trigger reminder emails.
Add Tumor Board
After adding, click ✎ on any board to edit sites, subspecialties, and participants.
🏥 Clinics
Define recurring outpatient clinics. Each clinic sets a recurrence pattern (weekly, biweekly, monthly, custom), the sites/specialties it serves, and either a fixed participant list or a rotating roster (equal-frequency assignment). Clinics appear automatically on each assigned physician's calendar feed.
Add Clinic
After adding, click ✎ on any clinic to set recurrence, sites, specialties, participants, and optional rotation.
📤 Publish Schedule
Send each physician a personalized digest of their assignments for a date range. Each recipient sees only their own shifts; a brief summary of who covers what is shown only when explicitly enabled below. Recipients who have opted out of "Published schedules" notifications are skipped.

All rules are enforced during auto-assign. Conditions can restrict, require, or limit assignments at specific sites.

✍️ Custom
Type any rule in plain English. Surfaced as a warning during manual assignment and visible to the AI scheduling agent. Use the structured tabs above for rules the auto-assigner enforces.
Limit or block assignments at a specific hospital
Require a specific site when working on a certain day of week
Restrict what shift types this physician can work at a site
Control how many days in a row this physician can be assigned (globally)
📍 Per-Site / Per-Slot Sequence Rules
Constrain how many consecutive days the physician can work AT a specific site (or at a specific labeled slot within a site, like stfs1). Useful for "max 2 weeks in a row at the satellite site" or "no more than 3 consecutive Mondays at slot stfs1." Slot labels come from the IR Shift Slots admin page.
Apply scheduling rules tied to tumor boards or clinics this physician is part of. The conference list comes from the 🔬 Tumor Boards and 🏥 Clinics admin pages — pick "Any" to apply to all of them, or scope to a specific event.
–
 
 
 
Editing existing rule
When this physician is on call, force their day shift on the same date to a specific hospital. Auto-assign uses this to keep call coverage geographically consistent (e.g. if they take call, they read at the call site so they're physically present).
⚠ Tip: Pick "Any call type" if you want the rule to cover BOTH daily AND weekend calls. "IR daily call" ONLY fires on Mon-Thu daily calls. "IR weekend call" ONLY fires on Fri-Mon of a weekend. Most admins want "Any call type".
"Apply to shift kind" filters whether this rule gates DR shifts, IR shifts, or both. Pick "IR day shift only" to make the rule participate exclusively in the IR Shift auto-assign pool (e.g. "when on call, my IR shift must be at CHN, but DR shifts can be placed anywhere").
Date ranges when this physician cannot be assigned IR call (daily or weekend). Respected by auto-assign, rebalancer, and manual assignment. Use for planned unavailability not covered by vacation (e.g. conferences, board exams).
Vacations & Time Off
Vacation Sold Back: Weekdays in this period are automatically filled with DR Home shifts and blocked for IR assignment. The physician is considered "working" and available for DR read-from-home.
PhysicianTypeStartEndDaysNotes
📅 Vacation Picking
Annual 52-week vacation lottery. Physicians submit ranked preferences each round; assignments follow seniority order. Assigned weeks sync automatically to the vacation schedule.

Pick Groups Rotating-draft assignment for 2nd / 3rd shift coverage
Create a group, add members in draft order, then each member picks their weeks in turn. Multiple groups can run in parallel for different sections of the schedule. The rotation wraps so everyone picks roughly the same number of weeks. Members on vacation that week are auto-skipped to the next picker.

IR Shift Auto-Assign
Distributes configured IR shift slots to available physicians by FTE quota and ~70% anchor compliance over a 6-month period. On-call physicians are prioritised for a shift on their call day.

Select a month and click Preview.

IR Auto-Assign
1 physician per group covers Mon–Thu. Weekend call covers Fri→Mon. Physicians not on call are left unscheduled.

⚖ IR Call Rebalancer
Move assignments around to hit 100% FTE for every physician across a 6-month period. For calls: redistributes all Daily and Weekend IR calls. For shifts: redistributes existing procedure shifts or fills all available slots to FTE targets. Select a mode and click Preview.
⚓ Anchor Compliance
Shows what % of each physician's IR procedure shifts were at their designated anchor site. Target: ≥60%. Changes immediately when you pick a different month.

📅 Schedule
Create New Holiday
One slot per pool. Set = 1 for each pool that needs a physician covering the whole block. Leave 0 to skip that pool. IR groups below reflect the groups you've configured in IR Slots & Groups.
Assign Holiday Call
🔄 Sync Preassigned Holidays to Calendars
Runs automatically on every app load — every entry on this tab is pushed into the DR and IR calendars. Click the button to re-run manually (useful after direct-state edits or for verification). Idempotent and safe to repeat.
HolidayDateYearPhysician GroupNotesEditDel
FTE-weighted random lottery using the holiday definitions above. One physician per group covers the entire holiday block. Assignments sync to DR and IR calendars and clear any conflicting call already scheduled.
Generates assignments for one year at a time. Weighted mode considers career history from prior years — physicians who've been over-assigned get lower weight, owed physicians get higher weight, so long-run fairness emerges as you run the lottery each year. See the Counter tab for the multi-year career view.
Run Lottery
Only unassigned holiday slots for the selected year are filled. Already-assigned dates are skipped. Weighted mode uses career history from prior years to tilt the draw toward owed physicians.
Open Shift Board
Claim Board
Manage Shifts
Unclaimed shifts. Select your name to claim shifts you can cover. Anchor site matches are highlighted.

User Management
Manage registered accounts, create physician accounts, and manage physician linkages.
Create Physician Account
Create a login account directly for a physician and optionally link it to their profile. The physician will receive the credentials you set.
▶ One-time setup: deploy the create-user edge function
To create physician accounts directly (so they can log in without self-registering), you need a Supabase Edge Function with admin privileges. This function runs with the service role key so it can create auth users on your behalf, and verifies the caller is an admin before doing so.
⚠ Security: This function uses your service role key, which bypasses Row-Level Security. The function verifies the caller is an admin by checking Supabase Auth user metadata (role: 'admin') before performing any admin action. Never expose the service role key in the browser — it only lives as a secret on the edge function.
  1. In your Supabase dashboard → Edge Functions → Create function → name it create-user.
  2. Paste the Deno code below into the function editor.
  3. In the function's Secrets settings, add:
    • SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY = your project's service role key
      (Found in Project Settings → API → service_role secret — the one marked "secret")
  4. In function settings, ensure verify_jwt is enabled (default).
  5. Deploy the function.
  6. Make sure your own admin account in Supabase Auth has role: 'admin' in its user_metadata. If you're not sure, expand the "Bootstrap first admin" section below after deploying.
Deno source code:
Loading…
💡 After deploying: the "Create Physician Account" form above will work. Any error returned now includes a specific reason (caller not admin, email already registered, password too short, etc.) instead of the generic "Failed".
▶ Bootstrap first admin — grant yourself admin role in Supabase Auth
If you're seeing HTTP 403: Caller is not an admin when creating users, your Supabase Auth user is missing the role: 'admin' tag in its user_metadata. The app's local USERS array says you're an admin, but Supabase Auth doesn't know that. Fix it in one of three ways:
Method A — Supabase dashboard (easiest, ~30 seconds):
  1. Go to Supabase dashboard → Authentication → Users.
  2. Find your admin email, click the row.
  3. Scroll down to User Metadata (or "Raw User Meta Data").
  4. Edit the JSON to include "role": "admin". Example: 
    {
  "role": "admin",
  "first": "Your",
  "last": "Name"
}
  5. Save. Then sign out of RadScheduler and sign back in so your new token carries the admin role.
Method B — SQL editor (if you have many admins): 
-- Run in Supabase → SQL Editor. Replace the email first.
UPDATE auth.users
SET raw_user_meta_data = raw_user_meta_data || '{"role":"admin"}'::jsonb
WHERE email = 'your-admin@example.com';
Then sign out and sign back in.
Method C — Temporary open-bootstrap endpoint: Modify the create-user function to skip the admin check on a one-time call, promote yourself, then restore the check. Only use this if Methods A and B aren't available to you. Not recommended for production deployments.
🛠 Tools
Admin workshop — what-if simulations, custom rules, advanced reports, system logs.
🔮 Simulators (4)
⚙ Rules & config (4)
📊 Analytics & reports (5)
📜 Logs & ops (5)
🔮 What-if simulator
Project a hypothetical change without committing it. Useful for "if Dr. X took the week of Christmas off, who absorbs the work?" and "what would removing this physician break?" The simulation runs against the current state, reports who would absorb each affected slot, and identifies anything that would become unfillable. Nothing is saved — close the result to discard.
🎟 Vacation lottery
Rank pending vacation requests for an in-demand week (Christmas, July 4) by seniority + recency-weighted past-popular-week balance. Higher rank = more deserving. The result is a recommendation, not a commit; admins still approve each request individually.
🚫 Pair-conflict rules
Don't schedule physicians A and B together on the same day or same shift. Useful for training overlap, sub-specialty pairing, or interpersonal conflicts. Severity controls whether the manual-add path warns or blocks.
🚧 Site capacity caps
Maximum physicians simultaneously assigned to a given site/shift on the same day. Enforced at manual-add time and used by the auto-assigner as a hard exclusion. 0 means uncapped.
🎯 Per-shift sub-specialty needs
Coverage requirements: "1st shift at CHN needs at least 1 body imager." The auto-assigner awards a small cost bonus to candidates who fill unmet needs. Manual scans flag missing coverage.
⚙️ Schedule constraint settings
Practice-level tunables for the constraint engine. These apply to auto-assign AND manual rule violations.
No day shifts within N days after a weekend call. 0 disables.
Can't draft same person for two holiday blocks within N days. Default 3.
Hard reject below this; soft penalty between this and the soft target. Default = soft - 15.
Burnout thresholds
🤝 Cross-coverage finder
Pick a date / site / shift; get a ranked list of cross-group physicians who could cover, ordered by least-disruption (own-group target gap + drive time + day availability).
🔌 Integrations
Outbound webhooks, SMS notifications, email digests, and a public read-only schedule export. Server-side delivery for SMS / digest requires the send-notification edge function with Twilio + email-provider credentials configured.
📮 Outbound webhooks
Quick presets:
📱 SMS notifications
📧 Email digest
📋 Monthly report card
Operational health digest for admins: coverage completeness, fairness (Gini), swap activity, sick days, burnout flags, audit volume. Auto-emails every admin on the last day of the month if a digest cadence is enabled, or generate one on-demand below.
🌐 Public read-only schedule
Generates a self-contained HTML snapshot of the active calendar's month. Host this anywhere (intranet, shared drive) so non-login users see the schedule. Re-generate after any change.
🛡 Robustness checks
Built-in regression suite (date math, schema validators, save/load round-trip, fuzzy match, diff helper), named manual snapshots, and practice-config export / import.
🔒 Schedule lock
After you publish a month's schedule, lock it to block accidental edits. Locked months refuse mutations until an admin explicitly unlocks (logged in audit). Use this once distribution is final.
Currently locked
📣 Practice announcements
Post a memo that shows as a dismissible banner on every user's dashboard. Pick severity, body text, and how many days it stays up. Each user dismisses independently.
All announcements
🔥 Vacation overlap heatmap
52-week strip showing the percentage of physicians off each week of the year. Spot Christmas / July-4 / spring-break problem weeks before the auto-assigner has to compensate.
📈 Practice analytics
Monthly volume trends across DR / IR / weekend / call / holiday / vacation events for the selected year, plus a fairness audit showing physicians whose actual workload differs most from their expected FTE share.
🔮 Predictive suggestions
Surfaces who's "due" for weekend call / holiday based on a 6-month lookback. Useful as a starting point when you're picking a specific person for a specific slot. The auto-assigner already weights fairness, so this view's job is to make the manual pick faster.
📄 Annual report
Per-physician year-end summary: total DR / IR shifts, weekend calls, holiday days, vacation days, by month. Print-ready (uses the same print stylesheet as the calendars). Useful for year-end credentialing reviews and accreditation packets.
🔬 Data integrity scan
Detects orphaned references — assignments pointing to a physician that's been deleted, swap requests with deleted parties, open shifts claimed by missing users, etc. Runs automatically once per session after login; surface here lets you re-scan on demand and one-click clean up findings.
📜 Audit log
Last 500 admin mutations — physician add/remove, shift add/delete, auto-assign apply, holiday assign, swaps resolved, backups restored. Filter by action prefix; click a row to expand the detail JSON.
🐛 Error log
Unhandled errors and runtime warnings captured by the global error handler and the various render paths. Sharing this output with support is the fastest way to diagnose a problem.
📥 Bulk operations
One-shot administrative operations that don't fit elsewhere.
Need to import Excel, publish, broadcast, or use the AI scheduler? Use the sidebar navigation — those pages have their own dedicated workspaces.
⚡ Power tools
Advanced one-click utilities. These wrap the lower-level helpers installed in the page's script and document each capability with a tooltip.
📊 FTE Monitor
Track assigned call and shifts against each physician's FTE targets. IR call targets use finite-slot math (actual available slots ÷ group FTE × physician FTE) over 6-month periods.
Monthly
6-Month Period
📈 Career Tally
🗺 Commute Times
Running balance = cumulative (actual calls − FTE-proportional share). Positive = physician has done more than fair share; negative = owed calls. Used to equalize future scheduling.
⚙ Settings
🎨 Appearance
Live theme editor — every change applies instantly and persists in this browser. Click Reset to revert to the original look.
Quick Presets
#2563eb

#18181b
🔗 Supabase — Shared Database
Connect to Supabase to share data across all users in real time. Every schedule change syncs automatically. Get your credentials from supabase.com → Project Settings → API.

📲 Multi-device setup: After saving credentials here, click 💾 Save File in the top toolbar and host/distribute the downloaded HTML. The saved file embeds both the URL and the anon key, so any device opening it is auto-configured and goes straight to the login form. The anon key is public by design — actual data access is gated by your Supabase Row-Level Security policies, so make sure those are set up before sharing the file widely.
Status: Not configured
▶ First-time setup: create the database table
In your Supabase dashboard → SQL Editor → New query, paste and run: 
CREATE TABLE radscheduler (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, data TEXT);
ALTER TABLE radscheduler ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "public_all" ON radscheduler FOR ALL USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);
Then paste your Project URL and anon key above and click Save & Connect.
📡 Offline support (Service worker)
A service worker caches the app shell so RadScheduler loads instantly even with no network. Saves still need internet; reads work offline against the most recent cached copy. Setup: click the button below to download sw.js, then upload it to the same folder as index.html on your hosting (e.g. radsched.org/sw.js). The next time anyone opens the app, it auto-registers.
Status: Checking…
🗄 Backups & Restore
A snapshot of this practice is taken automatically once per day. Click Load list to browse available backups, then Restore on a specific date to roll back the live data to that snapshot. Restoring overwrites everything. Make a fresh manual save (toolbar → 💾 Save File) before restoring if you want to be able to roll back the restore itself.
🐙 GitHub Auto-Deploy
⚠ Disk-watch is the recommended writer. If .git-autopush.sh is running (launchd agent com.radscheduler.autopush), leave the auto-deploy checkbox below OFF — running both produces 409 conflicts when they race for the same SHA. Use this card for occasional manual deploys (the 🚀 Deploy now button) or to deploy from a device that can't run the disk-watch (phone, secondary laptop).
Push every save to a GitHub repo via the Contents API. Useful when the app is hosted on GitHub Pages — your phone / other devices see updates within ~30 sec of each save (GH Pages re-publish time).

One-time setup: create a fine-grained Personal Access Token with Contents: Read and Write permission on this repo only. Generate at github.com/settings/tokens. The token stays in this browser's localStorage — never synced to Supabase, never embedded in saved HTML files.
/
DR Subspecialties
IR Subspecialties
🗺 Drive Times — Google Maps
Enter your Google Maps API key to calculate commute times. The key is stored in your browser only — never synced to the shared database. Calculated times are cached and shared.
Calculates both legs of the DR 1st shift with actual traffic at departure time.
Drive times calculated for 1st shifts only (DR + IR). Both legs use real traffic:
DR 1st Shift — configures when DR physicians commute  ·  IR 1st Shift — configures when IR physicians commute
Round-trip = outbound + return (not 2×one-way). Auto-refreshes every Monday 5 AM.
1st Shift Departure & Return Times 24-hour format · used for daily traffic queries
Drive-time queries use these shift-specific departure / return times so traffic is computed at the actual commute hour, not a generic snapshot. Only in-person commuting shifts are listed — DR 2nd, DR 3rd, and DR Home are read-from-home and have no commute.
Shift Depart (home→hospital) Return (hospital→home) DR 1st Shift IR 1st Shift
📅 Per-day traffic — fetches real Google Maps traffic for each scheduled shift on its specific date/time (more accurate than the snapshot above).
📜 Retrograde refresh — backfills past shifts with shift-time-based traffic estimates. Maps doesn't expose true historical traffic; past timestamps are clamped to "now" so the result represents current traffic at each shift's hour-of-day. Useful for filling badges on past shifts that never had a daily fetch.
🤖 Auto-refresh future drive times — runs in the background while the app is open so the badge on every upcoming shift always reflects current traffic patterns at that shift's hour. First fire is 5 sec after login; subsequent checks every 30 min, but actually re-fetches only when the throttle interval has elapsed. Throttle is per-browser (uses localStorage) so multi-tab doesn't pile up duplicate Maps queries.
🔍 Look up drive time — fetch or view the traffic-adjusted drive time for any physician to any site on a specific date.
Hospital Sites
FTE Caps & Anchor Rules
% of all assigned shifts must be at anchor site
MCF is globally optimal under the configured cost weights (anchor mismatch, cross-group fill-in, on-call preference). Greedy is faster on huge rosters but can leave fairness on the table. Use Compare to see the metric delta before flipping.
Weekend Call Needs by Subspecialty (per month)
📬 Admin Alert Inbox
A single email address that gets CC'd on every outgoing notification (chat, swap, shift assignment, holiday, broadcast). Useful when you want an audit trail in a monitored inbox, or when admin accounts don't have real email addresses. Leave blank to disable.
🧠 Scheduling Rules — If / Then Logic
Plain-English rules that the auto-assign engines will enforce. Use the AI Scheduler page to write new rules (it will parse your sentence into a structured form automatically). Rules below are enforced for DR shifts, IR calls, and weekend calls.
Example: "If Dr. Chen is assigned at CHE, she must be on 1st shift."

Loading…

To add a rule, go to 🤖 AI Scheduler and type the rule in plain English. The AI will translate it into a structured rule and file it here.
Site Management
Hospital Sites
Full site editor — click ⚙ on any row to edit name, address, IR group, DR/IR specialties offered, and per-site IR anchors/fill-ins. Changes cascade through every shift, call, and assignment record that references the site.
Slot Configuration
Configure physician slot needs per site and shift type. Adjust by week. The auto-assign engine reads these values to determine daily demand.
IR Slots & Groups
Configure IR groups (practice subdivisions like North, South, regional pods) and the physician slot needs for each group's call and procedure coverage. Call slots = number of IR call physicians on a given day of week. Shift slots = number of IR physicians doing daily procedures at each site.
IR Groups
Every IR-eligible site belongs to a group. Physicians inherit their group from their primary site. Default groups are North and South; add more for complex practices.
Call Slots
Number of IR call physicians needed per site per day of week. Mon–Fri: covered by daily IR Call. Weekends (amber): covered by IR Weekend Call, not these slots.
Procedure Shift Slots
Number of IR physicians needed at each site for the daily procedure schedule (separate from call). Mon–Fri only; weekends handled via call.
Import from Excel
Upload Excel files to auto-fill 2nd/3rd shifts and vacations. Download templates to see the required column format.
Import 2nd & 3rd Shifts
Required columns: Date (YYYY-MM-DD), Physician (Last, First or ID), Shift (1st, 2nd, 3rd, or Home for DR; 1st for IR), Site, Subspecialty (optional), Notes (optional)
Import Vacations & Time Off
📅 Vacation Pick Sheet
Standard Format
Import from the vacation pick calendar Excel file (e.g. 2026 Vacation Pick - Published.xlsx). The reader finds the Calendar View tab automatically, walks each week's two-column block, and categorizes every entry it sees. Names are matched to your physician roster with a fuzzy search (handles typos like "Mohtshemi" → "Mohtashemi" and hyphenated surnames like "Mossa-Basha"). Anything we can't match cleanly is surfaced for manual mapping before you commit.
Year handling: the importer detects the year(s) inside the file and tells you up front. If the file was templated from a previous year (e.g. a 2026 schedule built from a 2025 template), set Target year above and every date will be shifted accordingly. Leave blank to use the file's own dates.
Block convention: each pick column represents a 9-day block running Saturday through the following Sunday. Vacations span the full 9 days so the surrounding weekends are blocked from call assignment leading into and out of the vacation. 2nd / 3rd shift assignments span the same 9-day block as continuous coverage. Site is forced to At Home / Remote for imported shifts (matches the practice's home-only policy for 2nd / 3rd).
Import categories
Required columns: Physician, Start, End (YYYY-MM-DD), Type, Notes

🤖 AI Scheduling Assistant
Describe scheduling tasks in plain English — the AI reads your data and applies changes live.
Quick Actions
Configure
What it can do
✦ Read & analyze schedule data
✦ Assign or remove shifts
✦ Add vacation / blackout periods
✦ Fill unscheduled days
✦ Analyze fairness & gaps
✦ Bulk schedule operations
Configure:
✦ Create & manage hospital sites
✦ Update physician FTE & eligibility
✦ Create holiday definitions
✦ Write if/then rules in plain English
  that the auto-assigner will enforce
Changes apply live and save to your database instantly.
AI Scheduling Agent claude-sonnet-4-6
AI Scheduler
Hi! I have full access to your schedule data and can analyze, find problems, or make changes directly.

⚙️ One-time setup required: To use AI features, add your Anthropic API key as a secret:
1. Go to supabase.com → your project → Edge Functions → ai-proxy
2. Click Secrets and add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = your key
3. Get a key at console.anthropic.com

Once set, ask me anything about your schedule!
Practice Management
Each practice has its own isolated data. Users are assigned to a practice when their account is created. Switching practices reloads all data from that practice\u2019s database row.
